_README: Edit this file to change website copy. Open on GitHub, click the pencil icon, change text inside the quotes, commit. Site rebuilds automatically. Keep the quotes and commas exactly as they are. To add a case study, copy a block in 'caseStudies' and change the fields.

apply.title: Tell us about your business.
apply.intro: We work with a small number of founders at a time. If the fit is right, we'll send a calendar link within one business day.
apply.successBody: We read every one. If there's a fit, we'll be in touch within one business day.

founder.name: Mohammed Saad Shaikh
bio: Saad started Daee after running growth for D2C and B2B brands across India and the Gulf — long enough to see the same patterns break the same companies. The studio is his answer to it.
He spends his time inside the work — running ad accounts, building CRM logic, writing the brief, reviewing creative. The agency model where the founder pitches and disappears doesn't run here.

philosophy.title: We work with founders who already know what they're doing.
body: We don't take retainers from companies that don't have product–market fit, can't service the leads they get, or want a vendor to ride. The system only compounds for operators who already have a real business and want it to grow without breaking.
If that's you, the work is plain: we sit beside the team, build the revenue infrastructure, run the spend, and own a number. We tune monthly. We replace ourselves with documentation, not lock-in.

caseStudies.indexIntro: We work with a handful of founders at a time — by design. Below: the cases we're allowed to share.

items.body: A meal delivery startup stuck on the engine. We rebuilt the funnel end-to-end — Meta Ads, ManyChat qualification, Zoho CRM, Conversion API feedback loop. 1,942 leads across 10 months. The founder eventually asked us to pause the campaign because the kitchen couldn't keep up.

Their ads worked — on paper. Leads came in, dropped into a CRM nobody opened, and sat there while the sales team chased Instagram DMs. We rebuilt the funnel end-to-end — landing path, CRM routing, tracking, creative loop. Sales started picking from a queue instead of chasing.
items.problem: They didn't have a lead problem.

items.body: We repositioned the advisory around the one client outcome that actually mattered and rebuilt the entire inbound system to match. New creative. New qualification. Fewer leads — and a much higher close rate.
High follower count, low revenue. We rebuilt the creative system around an offer structure the audience could actually buy into. Organic became the feeder for a paid system that closed.

hero.subhead: A founder-led agency for service businesses that deserve to grow. Meta Ads, funnels, CRM, creative direction — treated as one compounding system, not line items on separate retainers.

proteinPals._note: Long-form case study content. Heavily formatted — edit text inside quotes only.

footer.tagline: Revenue systems for founders who already know what they're doing.

services.title: Six parts. One system.
services.intro: Each of these is a component of one revenue system. Alone they leak. Together they compound. We don't sell them as separate retainers — and we only sign if the whole picture makes sense.

items.title: Meta Ads
items.body: Built around your funnel and your offer — not someone else's template. Creative loops, targeting and measurement, rebuilt monthly.

items.title: Creative direction
items.body: Ads and organic content that sound like you and convert like us. Written, scripted and art-directed against the system — not content for content's sake.

items.title: Lead generation systems
items.body: Forms, nurture, qualification. Set up so your sales team stops chasing cold leads and starts picking from a queue of warm ones.

items.title: CRM & automation
items.body: The invisible machinery behind the revenue. If you can't see the system, you can't improve it — and most teams can't see it.

items.title: Tracking & attribution
items.body: First-party, event-based, honest. Attribution that reflects how revenue actually moves — not what a platform tells you to believe.
